title: DIY Storage Cabinet
summary: Build a simple storage cabinet with a drawer and doors with adjustable shelves.
description: Build a simple storage cabinet with a drawer and doors with adjustable shelves. This easy-to-build versatile piece of furniture can be used in any room!

materials.name: Box of 1 ¼” pocket hole screws (See tools list)
Box of 2” pocket hole screws (See tools list)
Box of 2 ½” pocket hole screws (See tools list)
Paint or stain in the color of choice

steps.title: Watch The Video
steps.body: Check out the YouTube video for this project.

steps.title: Prepare the boards
steps.body:
Make the cuts per the cut list. Review the plans and make pocket holes where needed. Pocket holes in plywood are made with ¾” setting on the pocket hole jig and pocket holes in the 2x2 is made with 1 ½” setting.
Apply edge banding to all the edges that will be visible.

steps.title: Attach the sides
steps.body: Attach the sides to the bottom using 1 ¼” pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Attach the rails
steps.body:
The first set of rails is attached to the top of the cabinet using wood glue and 1 ¼” pocket hole screws.
The second set of rails is attached as shown below using pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Attach the divider
steps.body: Measure and mark the exact location of the center divider. Add the divider. Make sure it is square and level prior to attaching with pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Build the drawer box
steps.body:
Build the drawer box as shown below.
The drawer box can be built two ways - either with the bottom sitting in a dado groove or on the outside.
Install the drawer slide and make sure the drawer works before removing it for the next step.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Make sure that the top is well centered before attaching with pocket hole screws through the sides and countersunk pocket hole screws in the rails.

steps.title: Build the base
steps.body: Build the base using the 2x2 boards as shown.

steps.title: Attach the base
steps.body: Center and attach the base to the bottom of the cabinet frame using countersunk 2” pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Install drawer and door
steps.body: Install the drawer front as well as the doors using the cabinet door mounting jig set to the inset door setting.

steps.title: Attach the cabinet hardware
steps.body: Using the hardware jig attach pulls to the cabinet.

steps.title: Add the back
steps.body:
In order to anchor the cabinet to the wall, you can add back support.
Also, add a backer board to the bottom part of the cabinets using a staple gun.

**Tip:** When cutting the angles for the legs, leave the miter saw at the angle and make all the cuts at once. Be sure to check for square before attaching the rails.
